canonical_definition: In the provided literature, CLUTRR is characterized as a benchmark for evaluating relation inference. Its core task requires a model to deduce "familial relations from partial graph descriptions in natural language," as stated in one source. This benchmark is used to operationalize and measure specific reasoning abilities. For instance, one paper uses CLUTRR as a measure for both Logical reasoning and Complex reasoning. The consistent framing across the available sources positions CLUTRR as an instrument for assessing a model's capacity to infer relationships from textual descriptions of a graph.
papers: FlashAdventure: A Benchmark forGUIAgents Solving Full Story Arcs in Diverse Adventure Games
TRIAL: Token Relations and Importance Aware Late-interaction for Accurate Text Retrieval

definitions.text: A benchmark for relation inference that requires deducing familial relations from partial graph descriptions in natural language.
